page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance but weaker single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores fall into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, while multi-core metrics, such as the Geekbench multi-core score and Passmark CPU score, sit in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds, though the Passmark memory score is relatively weak. Single-threaded compression tasks perform poorly, whereas multi-threaded operations and database-like workloads, such as Redis SET operations, achieve average throughput.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the ecs.r5.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-thread CPU performance or local NVMe storage. The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, relational databases, and multi-threaded data processing tasks where RAM capacity is the primary bottleneck.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, contrasted by weaker single-core and single-threaded processing speeds. With a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, the ecs.r5.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand extreme single-thread performance. It is best suited for caching systems, relational databases, and multi-threaded enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive enterprise applications and database workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory (8 GB per core)
